note: Player-facing profile for every tradition: what the craft IS, what it can do at first rank, what mastery looks like, and what it costs. Written for two surfaces — the character-creation guide (pick a tradition knowing what you are choosing) and the Library. Function coverage is derived from the shipped ability content, not authored separately, so it cannot drift. Authored by Aevi (PO) during the SNG-147c per-rank pass, 2026-07-18.

traditions.name: The Harmonic
traditions.oneLine: Sound as structure. You hear what a thing is made of, and you can sing it steadier, quieter, or apart.
traditions.theCraft: Harmonics treat vibration as the honest layer of the world. Everything has a note — a wall, a crowd, a wound, a lie — and a practitioner who finds it can reinforce it, mask it, or break it at the point where it was always going to fail.
traditions.atCreation: You perceive through sound where others need light: bodies through a wall, a digger at work below, the gait of someone you know. You can push objects at a distance, blanket a corner in quiet, or run on a rhythm that carries you further than your legs should.
traditions.withMastery: A chord set once and left ringing that holds a company through a night's march. A room brought to genuine willingness by a voice. A shatterpoint called on a Radiant lattice, a fortress wall, or a body — and struck where it was already going to fail.
traditions.theTrade: Nothing a Harmonic does is quiet. The craft is audible by definition; there is no stealth in it. It works on structure, so it is superb against the made and the massed and awkward against the singular and the strange.
traditions.whoPractices: Choir-trained, mostly. Harmonic and Radiant are closed to each other — a life spent learning to hear is not a life spent learning to see.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Radiant reveals by exposure; Harmonic reveals by resonance. The Radiant makes a thing visible. The Harmonic makes it audible, and then makes it fall.

traditions.name: The Radiant
traditions.oneLine: Light as tool, weapon, and witness. Nothing you shine on gets to stay hidden, and at full focus the beam does more than show.
traditions.theCraft: Radiants work light the way a smith works metal: stored, shaped, thrown, and sharpened. The tradition's premise is that exposure is a good in itself — that what survives being seen clearly is what deserved to.
traditions.atCreation: You read the hidden spectra — heat on a handle, tampering under paint, the flush of a lie in good light. You bend light to hide in, bank daylight in a jar against the night, and coax a season's warmth into a struggling seedbed.
traditions.withMastery: A lattice fence warding a compound for a season. Surgery done by your own operating glow, deep enough to rebuild a crushed hand. And the Sunlance — a single coherent strike through a stone wall, an engine block, or a person.
traditions.theTrade: You glow. There is no Radiant stealth, no Radiant ambush, no Radiant anonymity — the craft announces its user in the act of using it. It fails on mirror-finish, on running water, and in true dark where there is nothing to work with.
traditions.whoPractices: Glasshome-trained, and the blaze-adjacent settlements. Closed to Harmonic, and the two traditions have opinions about each other.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Where the Harmonic finds a thing's note, the Radiant simply shows it. Both end arguments; only one of them does it politely.

traditions.name: Precursor Work
traditions.home: the Old Roads and the lattice under everything
traditions.oneLine: The machinery the world was built on still answers, if you learn its grammar. Never offered at creation — earned in fiction.
traditions.theCraft: Precursor work is not a tradition so much as a conversation with what the builders left running. It is queried, not commanded; it answers on its own terms; and every exchange pulls the practitioner's own coordinate further toward the pole they are drawing from.
traditions.atCreation: Not available. Access opens per-ability through play — a remnant found, a road mastered, a teacher who owes you. Substrate-born origins are seeded a single innate base and grow it like any skill.
traditions.withMastery: Reading a thing's unrepeatable cosmic address and navigating to it across any distance. Foreclosing a possibility outright — and Hold the Aperture, the standing refusal that keeps open what the lattice is closing.
traditions.theTrade: Drift. Every use moves your vector, and Foreclose moves it toward the contraction the framework names as the enemy. The engine tracks it. The GM will eventually name where it has taken you.
traditions.whoPractices: Nobody by right except the substrate-born, and even they only hold the doorway. Everyone else earns each ability separately.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Its living counterpart is the green current; its tangled counterpart is the wild. Same depth, different substrate, and they do not mix.

traditions.name: The Green Current
traditions.home: the Quickwood and every rooted place
traditions.oneLine: The life-layer the Rootkin never surrendered. The ground is awake, and it will answer a true touch.
traditions.theCraft: Where the Precursors built a substrate, the Rootkin kept the one that grew. The green current runs through everything living and connects every rooted place to every other. It answers sincerity and refuses force.
traditions.atCreation: Not available. Innate to rootkin origins, seeded per-ability; earned by everyone else through the Reach.
traditions.withMastery: Waking a whole district's current so that ground you have quickened carries a party rested. Travelling the green road between rooted places, carrying others, toward places you have never walked because something living there knows your touch.
traditions.theTrade: It reaches only where life runs unbroken. A dead zone, a machine-city, a burned waste has no green road through it and no current to wake. And what you wake, you tend.
traditions.whoPractices: Rootkin by birth; Ashwardens and travellers of the Reach by long apprenticeship.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: The precursor lattice is fabricated and obeys grammar. The green current is grown and obeys relationship. Neither can do the other's work.

traditions.name: The Churn
traditions.oneLine: Both substrates at once, tangled and generous and never twice the same. It gives — just never exactly what you asked for.
traditions.theCraft: The fae art of productive disorder at the substrate layer. The Churn accretes and improvises. Its gifts are real, potent, and shaped by the current rather than the asker.
traditions.atCreation: Not available. Innate to churnfolk and abyssal origins; opened per-ability.
traditions.withMastery: Naming the shape of what you want and letting the Churn invent every detail. A district-wide flowering of growth-and-ruin that lasts days before the tangle takes it back. Bargains struck with places rather than persons, set to outlive both parties.
traditions.theTrade: No precision, ever, at any rank. You cannot aim it at pure growth or pure ruin. And the Honest Bargain is scrupulously fair, which is the horror rather than the safety.
traditions.whoPractices: The Wild Half — churnfolk, fae, and the Abyssals who kept their hollow.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: It is the third substrate. Precursor work is grammar and green current is relationship; the Churn is neither, and both.

traditions.name: The Somatic
traditions.home: the Flesh-Temples and the Somatic Reaches
traditions.oneLine: The body as a mastered instrument. Not strength — control, past where control should end.
traditions.theCraft: Somatics train the physical self until it answers the will completely: breath, heartbeat, pain, fear, and the precise economy of motion.
traditions.atCreation: Endurance and poise past the ordinary. Control of breath and heartbeat to steady fear, pain, or cold. Motion precise enough to read as grace.
traditions.withMastery: Surviving what kills others. Crossing terrain that stops everyone else. And the whole mastered frame behind a single strike — stance, breath, and timing arriving together.
traditions.theTrade: It is control, not raw force, and it is not thought-work. A Somatic who leans on Second Wind too long is borrowing at a rate the Flesh-Temples consider usurious.
traditions.whoPractices: Flesh-Temple trained. The Middle Way braids it with Noesis; the pure poles cannot.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Noesis is the same discipline pointed at the mind. The Whole Act is what happens when someone refuses to choose.

traditions.name: The Cogitant
traditions.oneLine: Thought sharpened until it acts on the world. Sharp, not wise — the Cogitarium is clear about the difference.
traditions.theCraft: Memory, focus, and reason trained to instrument-grade. A Cogitant models systems and people completely enough to predict them, and holds structure under conditions that break other minds.
traditions.atCreation: Perfect recall of what you attend to. Focus that ignores pain and distraction. Reasoning faster and further than the unpracticed.
traditions.withMastery: A life you can walk back through. Function under cold, injury, grief, and weeks of pressure until the work is done. And thought turned on a person: a conclusion delivered exactly where their reasoning rests, which staggers a mind the way a blow staggers a body.
traditions.theTrade: Sharpness is not wisdom, and this craft least of all. It needs a mind that reasons — the disciplined, the genuinely mad, and the simply uninterested all shrug it off. What you take apart in someone this way, you are responsible for putting back.
traditions.whoPractices: Cogitarium-trained, and the self-taught who found the discipline alone and usually paid more for it.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Soma answers the will through the body; Noesis through the mind. Both are the same refusal to accept an ordinary ceiling.

traditions.name: Wildcraft
traditions.home: the Churn and the unspooling places
traditions.oneLine: Improvisation as power. You are best exactly where the plan has stopped working.
traditions.theCraft: The Churnfolk discipline of productive disorder — riding luck, reading the grain of a collapsing situation, and denying an opponent the predictability they were counting on.
traditions.atCreation: Turn a chaotic moment to advantage. Improvise a working solution from whatever is to hand. Make one plan — yours or theirs — come apart usefully.
traditions.withMastery: Unmaking order at scale: a fortress's discipline, a system's certainty, a plan a season in the making, all coming apart at once — and out of the loosed chaos, the one opening that could not otherwise have existed.
traditions.theTrade: Nothing repeatable. It will not work the same way twice, it offers no fine control, and it cannot give you a result you specified in advance.
traditions.whoPractices: Churnfolk, and anyone whose life has taught them that plans are a kind of superstition.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Latticework is its exact opposite and its best opponent. Each is strongest where the other is blind.

traditions.name: Latticework
traditions.oneLine: Structure as power. What you bind stays bound; what you predict, you have already half-defeated.
traditions.theCraft: Order-craft: plans that hold under pressure, systems read to their load-bearing point, possibilities bound down until they are reliably true.
traditions.atCreation: Build a plan that holds. Predict a system's next move. Impose enough order on a mess to act cleanly inside it.
traditions.withMastery: Structure imposed at scale — a lawless place made orderly, a chaotic force predicted and countered, a possibility foreclosed by sheer structure.
traditions.theTrade: It cannot improvise and it cannot handle the genuinely novel. Anything that must breathe or grow resists it, and Wildcraft is built specifically to make it brittle.
traditions.whoPractices: The Lattice-Cities, their courts, and their engineers.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Wildcraft rides collapse; Latticework prevents it. Riding Order is the braid — unmake a structure and re-bind the falling pieces in the same motion.

traditions.name: The Mason
traditions.oneLine: The real is what you can touch, weigh, and set. Everything else is a claim awaiting proof.
traditions.theCraft: Thingcraft: material read by hand, structures set so they stay, and a flat refusal to grant abstraction any purchase on the real.
traditions.atCreation: Know a thing's true weight, material, soundness, and worth by touch — no lie of appearance survives your hand. Find the gap that is in every wall.
traditions.withMastery: The keystone blow, and where there is no keystone, one manufactured. Whole structures unmade back to raw material. And in your presence, illusion and symbol-working simply fail: the thing is what it is.
traditions.theTrade: The Masons' great blindness is that some things genuinely are not reducible to a touchable fact. The tradition is not good at admitting this.
traditions.whoPractices: The Given Land's builders, and skeptics everywhere.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Formcraft holds that matter is the coarse residue of pattern. The Mason's Denial defeats it outright, which is exactly why Figurists find Masons so tiresome.

traditions.name: The Figurist
traditions.oneLine: Pattern is the finer layer of the real. Draw the figure truly and the world briefly consents to match it.
traditions.theCraft: Formcraft: seeing what a thing is an instance OF, then making symbols that act — marks that do in the world what they mean.
traditions.atCreation: See the pattern behind the fact. Read a set of particulars and name the form they instance.
traditions.withMastery: A standing figure that holds a room to its pattern as long as the mark is undefaced. A figure sent walking with an errand and enough form to run it. And the true name — a handle on a thing's very being, which is also a handle it has on you.
traditions.theTrade: It needs the touchable to be secondary, so a Mason present ruins everything. A false or sloppy figure binds nothing and warns the thing you tried.
traditions.whoPractices: The Pattern Reach, the Lattice courts, and the Cathedral's more dangerous scholars.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Mason and Figurist are the same argument conducted for centuries. Neither has won and both are correct.

traditions.name: Umbracraft
traditions.oneLine: Sight without light, and sanctuary for what the light would destroy. Not evil — hidden, which is a different thing.
traditions.theCraft: The dark-pole mastery: concealment past a knowing searcher, true sight in lightlessness, shadow worked as a material, and the harboring of what should not be exposed.
traditions.atCreation: See clearly where there is nothing to see by. Wrap yourself and a small space in concealment. Put out a lamp at distance.
traditions.withMastery: Shadow commanded at scale — a caravan, a rite, a rebellion hidden. Sanctuary the Blaze cannot breach. And the shadow road: league upon league between distant darks, arriving where no light has been.
traditions.theTrade: Unshadow undoes it, and a Blazeborn at full radiance undoes it everywhere at once. The craft protects and hides; it does not win a direct exchange with someone who can simply make the room bright.
traditions.whoPractices: The Umbral Depths, the sheltered, and everyone who has needed to not be found.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: The umbral and the blazeborn are exact mirrors — each has an ability that makes their extreme into food, a healing that works only in their medium, and a road that runs on their element. They are the same person raised in opposite places.

traditions.name: Radiance
traditions.oneLine: Revelation as a moral position, and a beam that ends what should not continue.
traditions.theCraft: The light-pole mastery: exposure that strips every concealment at once, light worked as material, and the full blaze — which is where the tradition stops being about seeing.
traditions.atCreation: Throw light that shows things as they are — no illusion, no disguise, no shadow holds. A focused beam that can burn or blind.
traditions.withMastery: A whole deception undone at once, a hidden thing dragged into the light before a people, and a beam that ends the unendurable. Declarations made in full blaze that those who witnessed cannot pretend they did not see.
traditions.theTrade: You are always visible, and revelation does not discriminate — it exposes allies, and it exposes you. A truth burned free cannot be put back.
traditions.whoPractices: The Radiant Wastes, where intense light is home rather than hazard.
traditions.acrossTheAxis: Umbracraft harbors; Radiance exposes. The Blazeborn believe what survives being seen is what deserved to. The Umbral know exactly who that argument has historically been used against.
